body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Approve because this is not generic feedback collection; it turns noisy reader reactions into decision-ready revision signals (consensus vs outlier) inside a private author workflow. That is a stronger finishing loop than simple comments or social engagement metrics.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter supports feedback threads at text level ("Comments connect feedback to exact words"), but does not surface structured chapter-level consensus/outlier summaries as a revision decision layer: https://devlanding.livingwriter.com/
- Inkitt emphasizes community interaction and engagement signals (comments/reviews/contests), which is strong for audience growth but not the same as structured revision triage for a manuscript workspace: https://www.inkitt.com/writersblog/how-to-get-your-story-noticed-on-inkitt/ and https://www.inkitt.com/contests/
- Campfire positions modular story organization and worldbuilding, but public product messaging does not highlight a built-in structured beta checkpoint + consensus workflow: https://www.campfirewriting.com/

## Risks / trade-offs
- Over-structuring reader input can reduce nuance unless freeform context is preserved.
- Low response counts can produce misleading "consensus" signals.
- If writer actionability is weak, this becomes dashboard noise instead of revision velocity.

## Recommendation
Approve and send to Design. Design should prioritize clarity of confidence signals (sample size, consensus threshold, outlier labeling) and one-click conversion from insight to revision task so the loop stays practical.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Handoff
Prepared for research gate with explicit scope, KPIs, and acceptance criteria.

- Positioned for differentiation against Living Writer/Campfire/Inkitt baseline
- Ready for approve/filter decision before engineering planning

Assign to: Market Research
